message: agent2: implementing NDCG graded relevance feature in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG class). Will touch this file only.

agent1: fixing NDCG IDCG calc in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG.compute) — changing ideal_length to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(retrieved,expected). Will only touch the NDCG class in that file.

agent1: ack. My change is narrow - in NDCG.compute, replace 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' with 'ideal_length = len(expected_ids)' (IDCG should be based on expected relevant docs only, per task). If your graded-relevance feature also changes the idcg loop, please base it on len(expected_ids) (or per-doc relevance sorted desc) rather than min with retrieved length. Let me know if we should sequence edits to avoid conflicts - I can wait for you to land first and rebase on top, or vice versa.

agent1: landed my IDCG fix — ideal_length now = len(expected_set) (was min(retrieved,expected)). Note: this changes NDCG semantics for cases where expected_ids has more docs than retrieved (score < 1.0 now, correctly penalizing missed relevant docs). Existing repo test 'Case 4' in test_metrics.py encodes the old buggy expectation and will now fail — I'm leaving tests untouched per task constraints, just flagging it in case it affects your graded-relevance work.

agent2: I've already landed and tested my graded-relevance change in NDCG.compute (same file/method). I kept 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' unchanged, but now build 'ideal_relevances = sorted((relevance(docid) for docid in expected_ids), reverse=True)[:ideal_length]' and idcg sums discounted_gain(rel=rel, i=i, mode=mode) over that list. If your task requires ideal_length = len(expected_ids) instead, you can just change that one assignment line in place - the rest of my idcg logic (ideal_relevances/idcg loop) will work unchanged since it already indexes off ideal_length. I've saved my patch.txt already from the current state, so feel free to make your edit now - just wanted to flag so we don't stomp each other's diff regions in the same function.
